sections.subtitle: Match Overview and Context
sections.text: Monza will host Atalanta at the U-Power Stadium in Monza on May 4, 2025, at 13:00 for Round 35 of the Serie A season. Monza, currently sitting at the bottom of the table in 20th place with only 15 points, face a daunting task against Atalanta, who are in impressive form at 3rd place with 65 points. This significant 50-point gap highlights the disparity between the two sides. The match will be officiated by Luca Pairetto, who averages 5.0 yellow cards and 0.38 red cards per game, suggesting a potentially feisty encounter.

sections.subtitle: Recent Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Atalanta have dominated their recent encounters with Monza, winning all of the last 5 head-to-head matchups. In their most recent clash on October 30, 2024, Atalanta secured a 2-0 victory at home, with Lazar Samardžić earning Player of the Match honors with an impressive 8.4 Sofascore rating. Atalanta have been the first to score in all 5 of these matches and have kept Monza without a clean sheet in each game, underlining their historical superiority in this fixture.

sections.subtitle: Team Form and Streaks
sections.text: Monza are in dire straits, with a streak of 5 consecutive losses and no wins in their last 14 matches. They’ve also failed to score in their last 3 games and haven’t kept a clean sheet in 9 outings, often conceding first in 4 of their last 5 matches. In contrast, Atalanta are unbeaten in their last 3 games and have a strong record of limiting matches to under 2.5 goals in 6 of their last 6 away fixtures, indicating a controlled and defensively solid approach on the road.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Atalanta
sections.text: Atalanta boast some of the league’s top talents, with Mateo Retegui leading the scoring charts with 24 goals, earning him the 1st place in the league rankings for goals scored and a 7.33 average rating (4th in the league). Ademola Lookman is another standout, ranked 4th in the league with 13 goals, alongside 5 assists, 57 key passes (2nd in the league), and 10 big chances created (4th in the league). Charles De Ketelaere also contributes with 5 goals, 5 assists, and 11 big chances created (3rd in the league), while Raoul Bellanova’s 8 assists rank him 2nd in the league for creative output.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch - Monza
sections.text: Monza will rely on forward Dany Mota, who has scored 5 goals and provided 2 assists this season, with a solid expected goals value of 4.92. Midfielder Georgios Kyriakopoulos has also chipped in with 2 goals and 4 assists, showing creativity with a 2.52 expected assists value. Players like Samuele Birindelli and Pedro Pereira will need to step up in midfield, with Birindelli contributing 2 goals and Pereira adding 1 goal and 3 assists, though their average ratings hover around 6.9 and 6.64, respectively.

sections.subtitle: Team Statistics and Tactical Insights
sections.text: Monza struggle offensively, with only 25 goals scored this season (19th in the league), and defensively, conceding 59 goals (19th for least goals conceded). They rank 20th in big chances created (38) and clean sheets (3), though they convert big chances at an impressive 42.1% rate (3rd in the league). Atalanta, on the other hand, have a prolific attack, with goal distribution showing strength in the 31-45 minute period (14 goals, 22.6% of total). Their matches often see under 10.5 corners, as seen in 5 of their last 6 away games, aligning with Monza’s low corner average of 3.68 per game (19th in the league).

sections.subtitle: Missing Players Impact
sections.text: Monza will miss key defenders Armando Izzo (1 goal, 1 assist) and Andrea Carboni, along with midfielder Matteo Pessina, which could further weaken their already fragile backline. Atalanta are without defenders Sead Kolašinac (2 assists) and Stefan Posch, but their depth in attack and midfield, bolstered by stars like Retegui and Lookman, should mitigate these absences. These missing players could tilt the balance further in Atalanta’s favor, especially given Monza’s defensive woes.

sections.subtitle: Disciplinary and Set-Piece Trends
sections.text: Monza rank 5th for most fouls committed (478) and 19th for least yellow cards received (82), averaging 2.41 per game, which could spell trouble against referee Luca Pairetto’s high card average. Atalanta’s matches often see fewer than 4.5 cards in 4 of their last 5 away games, suggesting a more disciplined approach. Both teams historically see under 10.5 corners in 4 of their last 5 head-to-heads, aligning with Monza’s poor ranking of 19th for corners per game (3.68).
